snippet.title: Bun B, Arian Foster, Kendrick Perkins & Lil’ Keke Talk Tom Brady, Lebron James & the Music Industry
snippet.description:
SUBSCRIBE ➡️ https://www.youtube.com/c/IAMATHLETE?sub_confirmation=1
WATCH MORE ➡️ https://www.youtube.com/c/IAMATHLETE

I AM ATHLETE | Season 3: Episode 17
Brandon Marshall and D.J. Williams welcome Bun B, Arian Foster, Kendrick Perkins and Lil’ Keke to the show as they discuss Tom Brady and Aaron Rodgers, as well as the good, the bad and the ugly when it comes to the music industry. #BunB #KendrickPerkins #LilKeke

Arian Foster opens up saying he believes Barry Sanders is the G.O.A.T when it comes to the NFL. The group immediately responds with their opinions starting out with D.J. explaining how Tom Brady is an absolute leader and has the seven rings to back him up. Bun B jokes around and says that D.J. has no idea what kind of battery he is putting in Keke’s back right now since Lil’ Keke is a big Tom Brady fanatic. He says “You’re the energizer bunny right now.” 

Brandon asks the group which musical artist is most similar to Tom Brady, as well as Aaron Rodgers. Bun B explains how Tom Brady is most related to Jay Z, while Aaron Rodgers is most similar to Naz. He explains how most new quarterbacks inspire to be Tom Brady, similar to rapper inspiring to be Jay Z. He says Aaron Rodgers is most similar to Naz because he doesn’t love the spotlight and then he goes back in the shadows when he’s off the field. 

Bun B shifts the conversation forward and talks about how easy it is nowadays to get your music to the consumers. He explains how Jay Z used the rap to perpetuate other business details that made him a billionaire. The same thing happened to P Diddy. Bun B says that most people would rather be famous, than be rich. Brandon laughs and says that they might need to break down this show and turn it into a Part 2.

snippet.title: Russian Army Tooth to Tail Ratio (Short)
snippet.description:
The link to the long video can be found here:
https://youtu.be/UmlGphUE9Xc

Tooth to Tail (T3R) measures the "Teeth" or the ratio of combat soldiers to the logistical "Tail" of support soldiers.  This can can be used as a measurement of the logistical and expeditionary capabilities of an army.

Russian Armored Brigades are very heavily laden with artillery and anti-aircraft weapons.  This creates an expanded logistical footprint which can't be serviced properly by 3 logistical battalions.

Although the Russians do not publish their T3R, a ratio of 1:1.1 can be assumed based on estimated personnel numbers.

NOTE: I know you Civil War guys are gonna get all FARBY on me. But let me explain. I’m sure you know that men back then were a heartier and more Capable breed of soldier.￼ The average man knew how to pluck a chicken, build a fire and forage for food. At any given time up to 20% of soldiers were tasked out with other duties beyond their primary duty of fighting. So more of the logistical burden was placed on the average soldier sho could somewhat meet this task rather than relying on specialized soldiers like we do today.

References:
  The Other End of the Spear: The Tooth to-Tail Ratio (T3R) in Modern Military Operations by  John J. McGrath    

The Russian Way of War
Lester W. Grau and Charles K Bartles.

Defeating the Battalion Tactical Group
CPT Nicolas J. Fiore  

Notes on Civil War Logistics: Facts & Stories 
By Rodney C. Lackey

Improving the DoD’s Tooth-to-Tail Ratio
By:
Jacques S. Gansler and William Lucyshyn

Moody is now behind bars at the same jailing facility.

snippet.title: Title vs. Deed: Don't Get These Legal Concepts Confused!
snippet.description:
Get your free guide: How to Have a Smooth Closing and Turn Past Clients Into Referral Sources
https://thomasandwebber.com/free-resource/

Buying a property in North Carolina? Schedule your closing at Thomas & Webber here:
https://thomasandwebber.com/schedule-a-closing-with-thomas-and-webber/

Need help with an eminent domain case in NC? Call us immediately: 704-663-1600

If you're new to my channel, my name is Tiffany Webber. I'm a real estate attorney and co-founder of Thomas & Webber, a real estate law firm serving the Lake Norman area of North Carolina. We've closed nearly 10,000 real estate transactions across our three offices in Mooresville, Huntersville, and Denver, NC.

I make free YouTube videos because buying a home should be a happy experience. People shouldn't be confused when diving into one of the biggest purchases of their life. I want to share the legal information that will put your mind at ease before you sign your contract and buy a property.

How I got here…

2010: Graduated college at 20 years old and started working as a pharmaceutical sales rep. I thought that was my career path.
2014: Decided to go to law school. 
2016: Got a job as an attorney. Started practicing civil litigation.
2020: Co-founded Thomas & Webber with my late law partner, Ben Thomas. We wanted to build a firm that actually communicated with clients and made real estate law accessible.
2021: Voted by my peers as North Carolina Weekly's Rising Star. Started serving on the board as the attorney for the Lake Norman Homebuilders Association.
2023: Voted by my peers as a Super Lawyers Rising Star (an award I've won in 2024 and 2025 as well).
2025: Invited to speak on Yahoo Finance as a live guest about the state of the real estate market. Our firm has now closed nearly 10,000 real estate transactions.

Today: I serve on multiple boards including the South Iredell Economic Development Committee, Iredell Economic Development Committee, and as board chair for Hope of Mooresville (a local non-profit). I've been invited to speak on national stages to share insights on real estate law with attorneys and real estate agents across the country.

Thomas & Webber operates three offices across the Lake Norman area. We handle residential real estate closings, estate planning, and business law. But what we're really known for is actually keeping our clients informed throughout the process—which shouldn't be revolutionary, but apparently it is.

Why do I make these videos?

Because I've seen too many people show up to closing confused, stressed, and blindsided by costs or terms they didn't understand. I've seen buyers lose thousands of dollars because they didn't know their rights during due diligence. I've seen property owners accept lowball offers from the DOT because they didn't know they could negotiate.

Real estate law shouldn't be complicated. It shouldn't require a law degree to understand what you're signing. And you shouldn't have to chase down your attorney just to find out what's happening with your closing.

So I'm putting everything I know into free videos—breaking down closing disclosures, explaining due diligence timelines, showing you how to protect yourself from wire fraud, teaching you your rights in eminent domain cases—all in plain English.

If you're buying or selling property in North Carolina, you're in the right place.

To everyone navigating real estate transactions: You're making one of the biggest financial decisions of your life. You deserve to understand every step of the process. You deserve an attorney who keeps you informed. And you deserve to walk into closing confident, not confused.

Keep learning. Keep asking questions. And never sign something you don't understand.

See you in the next video,
Tiffany

DISCLOSURE
Information shared here is for educational purposes only. This content does not create an attorney-client relationship. Individuals should consult with a licensed attorney about their specific situation. Real estate laws vary by state and change over time. Your results may vary.

snippet.title: What is the Russian Army Tooth to Tail Ratio?
snippet.description:
Tooth to Tail (T3R) measures the "Teeth" or the ratio of combat soldiers to the logistical "Tail" of support soldiers.  This can can be used as a measurement of the logistical and expeditionary capabilities of an army.

Russian Armored Brigades are very heavily laden with artillery and anti-aircraft weapons.  This creates an expanded logistical footprint which can't be serviced properly by 3 logistical battalions.

Although the Russians do not publish their T3R, a ratio of 1:1.1 can be assumed based on estimated personnel numbers.

NOTE: I know you Civil War guys are gonna get all FARBY on me. But let me explain. I’m sure you know that men back then were a heartier and more Capable breed of soldier.￼ The average man knew how to pluck a chicken, build a fire and forage for food. At any given time up to 20% of soldiers were tasked out with other duties beyond their primary duty of fighting. So more of the logistical burden was placed on the average soldier sho could somewhat meet this task rather than relying on specialized soldiers like we do today.

References:
  The Other End of the Spear: The Tooth to-Tail Ratio (T3R) in Modern Military Operations by  John J. McGrath    

The Russian Way of War
Lester W. Grau and Charles K Bartles.

Defeating the Battalion Tactical Group
CPT Nicolas J. Fiore  

Notes on Civil War Logistics: Facts & Stories 
By Rodney C. Lackey

Improving the DoD’s Tooth-to-Tail Ratio
By:
Jacques S. Gansler and William Lucyshyn

Rights:
"World War I Marines in a Trench, circa 1918" by Archives Branch, USMC History Division is marked with CC BY 2.0.

snippet.title: Inside The Virtual Australian Open | The Daily Forkast
snippet.description:
Welcome to The Daily Forkast, January 17th, 2021, presented by Angie Lau. For the latest in blockchain & crypto news. On today's show:

00:00 Coming up
00:33 Chinese University launches virtual campus
01:22 Korean lawmaker accepting crypto donations
02:05 Australian Open kicks off virtually

---
The Communication University of China has launched the country's first virtual university, allowing people a chance to visit and consider without having to go in person. The university developed the metaverse experience in its laboratory of virtual entertainment. And visitors can check it out via the Chinese search engine giant Baidu's XiRang Metaverse platform, which launched in December. The university has also begun hosting events, including 20th anniversary celebrations and in an awards ceremony at the virtual campus. 
Over in South Korea, in a first for the country, Democratic Party lawmaker Lee Kwang-jae has started accepting political donations in cryptocurrencies. Lee's website accepts donations in a variety of cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin, Ethereum and Korean internet giant Kakao's native token Klaytn. And according to the website, receipts will be issued in the form of NFTs. The lawmaker, who heads up the presidential candidate, Lee Jae-myung's Future Economy Committee, said that in order for policies on crypto in Korea to change awareness of blockchain technology in the political space needs to increase.
 
Meanwhile, as Novak Djokovic found out, it is not easy to travel to Australia for the tennis open this year, but you can still get a feel for the action by heading to court, tennis court, that is virtually. Visitors to the virtual version of Melbourne Park can chill out at the Beach House or check out the ballpark, just like in the real world. You can also watch classic matches or head courtside in the Rod Laver Arena to try your luck at earning rewards in a tennis game, which proved harder than maybe it ought to have been. Tennis Australia says that like with the real life event, their aim is to improve the experience every year. Whether it's providing people with tennis or music or shopping or brilliant restaurants on site. It's all part of an ecosystem that is so much more than just watching a ball go back and forth. Alongside being able to visit the virtual arena. 6776 unique art ball NFTs have been created each link to an individual plot on the court and offering not just unique artwork, but match statistics and the chance to win real championship balls. Those aspects added an interesting challenge for the developers. The initial art ball sales sold out within minutes, but many are now available for resale on the OpenSea marketplace. And the tournament continues in both the real world and the metaverse until January 30th. 
 
It's every investor's nightmare and a black eye for trust in the industry. In Hong Kong, customers of the Hong Kong-based crypto exchange Coinsuper have been unable to withdraw money or tokens since November. Police are now involved and there are now calls for broader regulatory oversight in the city. So how did this happen and what impact will this have on exchange activity in Hong Kong and beyond? Who better to ask than Henry Chong, CEO of Fusang Fusang, aims to reshape investment markets with blockchain technology.

Angie Lau: what does this say about the need for mandatory regulation in the digital space?

Henry Chong: I think it's critically important for regulators in jurisdictions everywhere to think about regulation. I think that the crypto industry has grown to the point where you can't ignore it anymore. You do need as a regulator to deal with these issues head on. I don't think people in the crypto industry should be worried about this. And a lot of people try to avoid regulation sometimes or we see it almost as a dirty word. But any game needs rules. That's what allows us to actually interact and play the game. That said, as important as it is for regulators to come in and be willing to regulate in a cogent fashion, I think it's just as important that individual investors also take responsibility for themselves and want to do business with platforms that are regulated.

---
#Crypto #Blockchain #BlockchainTechnology #DigitalAssets #Cryptocurrency #DeFi #Virtual #Regulation #AustralianOpen2022 #AO #Tennis #Metaverse #NFT #CryptoExchange #Gaming

snippet.title: 16 Lessons From 2022 - Joe Rogan, Jordan Peterson & Jocko Willink
snippet.description:
It's the end of 2022 and to celebrate I thought I'd run through some of the best lessons I've picked up over the last 12 months.

This year has had over 10,000 minutes of episodes produced so there was a lot to choose from but I ended up settling on 16 insights from some of my favourite conversations both inside and outside of the podcast.

Expect to learn why Jocko Willink thinks that most people overcomplicate motivation, what Joe Rogan taught me about difficult and valuable things, why Douglas Murray makes a mean manhattan cocktail, how Jordan Peterson highlighted the price you pay for inaction, how Rob Henderson predicts the news cycle with amazing accuracy, how Rick from Rick & Morty can help you be more confident and much more...

About Steve Trang

Steve Trang (https://www.stevetrang.com) is the founder of Real Estate Disruptors. He started his podcast in the middle of 2018 to inspire wholesalers and real estate agents to double their incomes by adding a 2nd leg to their business. The podcast has grown to tens of thousands of followers, with new community members sharing their success stories every week.

Steve also created the OfferFast Homes app. The app’s purpose is to help wholesalers jump-start their careers faster by putting all buyers in one place. The hope is to minimize the challenge of building a buyer list when getting started in wholesaling.

Steve’s legacy will be to create 100 Millionaires. One of his favorite quotes is from the great Zig Ziglar: “You can have everything in life you want if you will just help enough other people get what they want.” He heard this quote when he first got into real estate, and it has stuck with him throughout his entire career. It’s essentially one of the core values Steve lives by.

Since starting Stunning Homes Realty in 2013, the brokerage has had over 130 agents. In the greater Phoenix area, SHR has an almost 1% market share. Stunning Homes Realty sold one out of every 137 homes in the Phoenix metro (https://www.stunninghomesrealty.com).

snippet.title: How to Crush Law School Final Exams and the Bar Exam
snippet.description:
Crushendo offers innovative audio outlines, attack outlines, and practice questions to help you ace law school classes and the bar exam. Memorize with mnemonics, the method of loci, classical music, audio flashcards, and more. Say goodbye to inefficient law lectures and brick law school outlines. https://crushendo.com/

“I absolutely love Crushendo! It helped me get the best grades of my law school career so far. The illustrations, audio outlines, and mnemonic devices were especially helpful for preparing me for my closed book finals.” - JerMara Watkins

“Great product, only used it for my torts class so far, but I got a better grade in that class than any other! Will definitely use it for everything else going forward.” - Sheldon Bartholomew

“Crushendo has been a lifeline during 1L year! Can’t wait to keep using it during my last 2 years of school and to study for the bar. The outlines and audio make studying for finals more enjoyable and actually help make the voluminous materials memorable long past exams.” - Allison Martinez

“I used the property course during my 1L year (got an A), and have just started the Evidence course to prepare for my 2L finals. The courses provide me a new way to study while I am not in the library. I love listening while commuting or exercising, and the material definitely sticks. Crushendo’s courses are really helpful!” - McKay Randall

“I have found Crushendo so helpful in Law school . . . I love their method to remember the key points.” - Stephanie Buffaloe

“Crushendo is an innovative and disruptive product that is visually appealing, mentally stimulating, and helps you learn and RETAIN the foundational principles and concepts of the law for each course. The instructional design is very effective at breaking down complex concepts into digestible chunks aided by witty graphics and clever acronyms.” - Zachariah Haws, BYU Law School

“The stuff it tells you sticks because it’s showering you in every memory trick (mnemonics, loci, funny) ever discovered in the last two thousand years.” - Aaron Richardson, Smart Student Secrets

“It’s an amazing, unique, superior product.” - Lance Yoon, Georgetown Law

“very best way to memorize black letter law” - Ryan Heidt, ASU Law School

#lawschool #lawstudents #finals #exams

snippet.title: AVENGERS: Kang Dynasty & SECRET WARS: Everything EXPLAINED
snippet.description:
The Marvel Cinematic Universe is in the middle of a Multiverse Saga this is going to culminate in two Avengers films: The Kang Dynasty and Secret Wars. But who is this new MCU big bad? Why is Secret wars set to be the BIGGEST crossover event of all time? We explain how this event will connect to every other Multiverse Phase, and how we could see the debut of Doctor Doom!

Shop our MERCH Store Here → https://bit.ly/3ULXY8Y/

Go here → http://screencrush.com/
TikTok → https://www.tiktok.com/@screencrushnews
Like us → https://www.facebook.com/ScreenCrush
Follow us → https://twitter.com/screencrushnews
Get our newsletter → http://screencrush.com/newsletter/

Written and Edited by Pavel Terehovsky (https://pavel8866.wixsite.com/pavelt) pavel8866@gmail.com
Hosted by Ryan Arey (https://substack.com/@ryanarey)

#Avengers #KangDynasty #secretwars 

The biggest announcement from comic-con is that we're Kang Dynasty and Secret Wars! Both in 2025. 
These 2 Avengers movies will be the culmination of the multiverse saga. 
This is Kang’s multiverse, we just live in it. 

So now we finally know the direction the MCU is heading. Well at least officially, all of us sort of figured that it all leads to Secret Wars. But I guess having that confirmation just gives us this nice feeling that everything is going to be alright. 

Ok so The Kang Dynasty and Secret Wars are the new Infinity War and Endgame. And Kang is the big bad of the saga.  

But here’s the thing, the Kang Dynasty - as the name suggests - is a huge Kang story. But it’s not really about the multiverse. 

Secret Wars is all about incursions and the multiverse, and we all assumed for a while now that it will be the climax of phase 6. 
But Kang didn’t actually have a major role in Secret Wars. 

So you have to wonder how Dynasty leads into Secret Wars, and how much of the stories Marvel will change. 

We need to put on our Kavin Faige hat, and do some heavy theorizing to figure it all out. So let’s get nuts, shall we? 


Alright, before we get into all the theories - and we have A Lot of ideas - let’s first go over both stories from the comics. 
Since the MCU’s versions of Kang Dynasty and Secret Wars are going to be drastically different from the source material.  


The Kang Dynasty is a crossover event from 2001. 
Kang the Conqueror comes from the 30th century to conquer the world. This is one of Kang’s biggest stories, because he won. 

Kang doesn’t just want to rule because he’s evil, it’s because he’s trying to save Earth. He saw where every timeline leads, and they all lead to a dark future. This part will totally be adapted into the MCU, but with the multiverse twist. 

Kang joined forces with his son, the Scarlet Centurion, and they conquered the world. That’s the dynasty part of the story. 

Kang killed millions of people to prove that he’s not messing around. 
He held a gun to the whole world, unless they all surrender, he will kill even more people.  
The Avengers were forced to surrender and let Kang win.

Obviously, in the end the Avengers beat Kang and saved the world.
 
Still Kang won, and something similar will surely happen in Avengers the Kang Dynasty. 
But there is nothing about the multiverse in this story. Since Avengers 5 is the first half in the culmination of the multiverse saga, that means the MCU’s Kang Dynasty is going to be very different. 

Secret Wars on the other hand is all about the multiverse. 
Well, the 2015 version of the story.
It’s a little complicated, so let’s quickly explain the difference.

The are two versions of Secret Wars in the comics.  

The first Secret Wars happened in 1984. A group of heroes and villains from Earth were teleported to battleworld. This world was created by the Beyonder. A being who was fascinated by the potential of superheroes on Earth. So he created his own cosmic Squid Game.

Secret Wars is one of the most beloved events in Marvel comics, and that’s the story the Russon brothers keep saying that they want to direct. But it doesn’t seem like we are getting this version of Secret Wars.   

In 2015, Marvel did another Secret Wars story, but this time it was about the multiverse.  
Incursions were the big threat to the multiverse, they caused universes to collide and reality to collapse.  

Right before everything ended, Doctor Doom took the power of the Beyonders. They are these god-like beings who are responsible for the incursions. 

With this godly power, Doom created Battleworld, a patchwork world composed of the remnants of different universes. 

Doom made himself god, ruling Battleworld as God emperor Doom.

In the end heroes from different universes came together against Doom, and they restarted the multiverse. 

Since Avengers Secret Wars will conclude the multiverse saga, it’s a no-brainer that the movie will draw its inspiration from 2015 Secret Wars. 

But I’m sure you noticed, Kang doesn’t actually play a big role in Secret Wars.
